# [CHECKLIST TYPE] Requirements Checklist: [FEATURE NAME]

**Purpose**: Validate the completeness, clarity, consistency, measurability, and scenario coverage
of the feature requirements before task generation

**Created**: [DATE]

**Feature**: [Link to spec.md]

**Audience/Timing**: [Author or reviewer; pre-task requirement-quality gate]

**Note**: This checklist is generated by `$speckit-checklist`. It tests the quality of the written
requirements; it MUST NOT test whether an implementation works or whether a task is complete.

<!--
  Replace every sample item below with feature-specific requirement-quality questions. At least
  80% of generated items must reference a specification section or use a Gap, Ambiguity,
  Conflict, Assumption, or Dependency marker. Mark an item complete only when the requirement text
  objectively satisfies it.
-->

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are all behaviors needed for the bounded stakeholder outcome specified? [Completeness, Spec §Bounded Outcome]
- [ ] CHK002 Are exclusions and non-goals explicit enough to prevent unrelated document types, administrative capabilities, and implicit legacy compatibility from entering scope? [Completeness, Spec §Exclusions and Non-Goals]
- [ ] CHK003 Are required Company-context, failure, duplicate, timeout, recovery, and terminal-outcome requirements documented for every applicable flow? [Gap]

## Authority and Evidence Quality

- [ ] CHK004 Are applicable legislation and official SRI sources identified by exact version or date? [Traceability, Spec §Authority and Evidence]
- [ ] CHK005 Are legacy references clearly identified as historical evidence rather than target authority? [Consistency, Spec §Authority and Evidence]
- [ ] CHK006 Are source conflicts resolved by the constitutional authority order and documented with their resolution? [Conflict, Spec §Source Conflicts]
- [ ] CHK007 Are unknown or contradictory behaviors registered as Pending Functional Validation without inferred answers? [Completeness, Spec §Pending Functional Validation]

## Requirement Clarity and Terminology

- [ ] CHK008 Can every MUST requirement be objectively interpreted without vague qualifiers or hidden implementation assumptions? [Clarity, Spec §Functional Requirements]
- [ ] CHK009 Are target terms consistent with the English canonical terminology mapping, with exact SRI terms confined to their approved context? [Consistency, Spec §Terminology Mapping]
- [ ] CHK010 Are monetary scale, rounding mode, precision, currency, boundaries, and calculation or reconciliation responsibility specified wherever monetary rules apply? [Completeness, Spec §Domain Rules and Invariants]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK011 Does every user story define an independently observable outcome and acceptance scenarios traceable to requirements? [Measurability, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing]
- [ ] CHK012 Are success criteria quantitative or otherwise objectively verifiable without selecting implementation technology? [Measurability, Spec §Measurable Outcomes]
- [ ] CHK013 Are critical fiscal, Company-scoping, sensitive-data, and failure outcomes represented in acceptance criteria where applicable? [Coverage, Gap]

## Scenario and Edge-Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK014 Are primary, alternate, exception, recovery, and non-functional scenario requirements complete for the bounded outcome? [Coverage, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing]
- [ ] CHK015 Are impossible dates, inconsistent totals, invalid catalogs, precision boundaries, and unsupported identifiers addressed where applicable? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK016 Are invalid or conflicting Company headers, duplicate commands, retries, timeouts, partial external failures, and reconciliation outcomes addressed where applicable? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Dependencies and Assumptions

- [ ] CHK017 Does each assumption identify an approved basis rather than replace an unresolved functional validation? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions and Dependencies]
- [ ] CHK018 Are external stakeholder, official artifact, and service dependencies identified with relevant availability or version constraints? [Dependency, Spec §Assumptions and Dependencies]

## Service Data Ownership

- [ ] CHK019 Is `X-Company-Id` the sole Company-context input, with exact cardinality, UUID, nil-value, normalization, placement, stable-error rules, request-body/input-schema exclusion, and any explicitly contracted response representation? [Completeness, Spec §Functional Requirements]
- [ ] CHK020 Is the immutable external Company UUID clearly distinguished from authentication, authorization, Company master data, and a fiscal snapshot, with draft-time snapshots excluded? [Clarity, Spec §Key Entities]
- [ ] CHK021 Are Company-owned aggregate/persistence/idempotency operations scoped by the authoritative identifier while immutable global SRI catalogs remain outside automatic Company scope, and are Company lookup/dependencies, authentication/authorization, Company administration, shared persistence, cross-service foreign keys or transactions, caching, background replication, and draft-time fiscal snapshots explicitly excluded? [Coverage, Spec §Exclusions and Non-Goals]

## Notes

- Replace these examples; do not retain generic items in a generated checklist.
- `[x]` means the written requirement quality criterion is satisfied, not that code was tested.
- Record unresolved findings inline and link them to the affected specification section.
- Checklist completion MUST be honest and MUST NOT be used to bypass clarification.
